title: Node File Services, Object Storage, and CDN Architecture · Mastery Check
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for node file services, object storage, and cdn architecture and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Node storage-architecture decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.

In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4447 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to select a typed control and data plane from trust, transformation, and volume evidence; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cost Failure Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cost Failure Model, keep Cost Failure Model connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
